item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Andrew Calhoun, 22, writes to tabloid Weekly World News, claiming that he had instantly grown a substantial mustache after playing the game Super Mario Bros.

The best investigative reporting on the planet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't think it was possible to make a videogame skip this good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think my favorite part was the PAC-Man one and the E.T part especially. Really good job at this SCP. I love gruesome transformations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heh, I thought that was supposed to be a visual pun on the last two characters of Samael's Hebrew name, סַמָּאֵל‬. Oops!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In The Ascension of Moses, the angel Samael is mentioned as being in the seventh heaven, so that's where I got that name.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
noice

any reason why the angel is 7H in Nier, or is that just to force it to fit the robot naming system in the game?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Both Calhoun and the reporter are thoroughly shaved and dosed with a Class-B amnestic.

Out of context quote of the month, right there.

I was concerned that this would turn into bad video game creepypasta, but the fact that you relied on the wtf-factor instead of "BLEEDING HYPERREALISTIC EYES" made it work. I have mixed feelings about the interview. On one hand, it's really well written, but on the other, it took away some of the initial impact of the logs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am so, so glad I decided to keep reading this to the end.

As much as I love video games, this particular object didn't hook me immediately for some reason. I don't really know what it was but there was something about the containment procedures that sort of just gave me the urge to go 'meh' and go back to the mainlist.

But I stuck it out, and holy hell, I love it so much. The descriptions of Samael's incarnations in the games he affected/possessed(?) were neat, I thought, and the effects, while mostly silly, I enjoyed enough that if the article had ended there without showing its whole hand, I'd have still upvoted.

But it showed its whole hand. And that made me love it so much more. The interview with the dev took away (almost) all of the mystery of this object, but in fantastic brow-raising fashion. The idea that the Atari video game burial was a Foundation coverup really got me good, and the rest of the reveal about almost all of what Samael was just gave me a wow moment that I usually only have with much more vast and heavy objects. That, and Hendricks' likable, believable character made this a perfect interview to me and a perfect close to this article as well.

I'd +2 if I could.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great, now I want some MEAT and SALT.

This took something that could have been lolFoundation or borderline -J and made it body horror. Good job!

I hope the Library got their book back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for pointing out these issues, I've gone in and changed the dates to make things a little more consistent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This pretty good, but a couple of issues:
The date on your last videogame entry is wrong (its 2003 for a 2017 game).

The Atari landfill was found and excavated in 2014. It was a pretty public spectacle, and it's very unlikely that the guy being interviewed wouldn't know about it and impossible that the Foundation wouldn't, considering the interview was done in 2016.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had an idea just like this one, where it was a gang of NPCs from one game that hopped around to other games to screw with people, and with an incident where everybody at the site turned into Mario.

Anyways, this was executed much better than I could have done it. Loved it in drafts, love it on the mainlist.

+1, you're on a roll

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hpsbj0OoeNo

Thanks to TheGreatSea, CaptainKirby, DrBalster, Hippo, DrChandra, not_a_seagull, Tuomey and Communism for their great feedback in chat!
